title: Access Recovery And Runtime Inventory
objective: Recover the documented workstation-to-Olares private-mesh path using the proven TermiPass named-pipe and Headscale registration recipe, and on success immediately capture the read-only host runtime inventory that Packet 001 was authorized but unable to collect.
dependency_note: Step 3 concluded that Packet 001 was the correct shape but could not complete because the workstation access path regressed. This packet is a bounded precursor that restores the proven mesh-access path using the documented 2026-05-01 recovery recipe and, on success, immediately captures the runtime inventory that Packet 001 could not obtain.
required_inputs: The access-recovery recipe documented under Verified Workstation Recovery in ops/agents/handoffs/2026-05-01-olares-private-stack-browser-terminal-bring-up-handoff.md
The Packet 001 partial result recorded in ops/agents/handoffs/2026-05-03-olares-phase-5-001-access-and-runtime-revalidation-handoff.md
The Step 3 decision that recommends this packet as the smallest next move in ops/agents/handoffs/2026-05-03-olares-phase-5-step-3-expansion-decision-surface-handoff.md
Current workstation access to the local TermiPass named-pipe API and the Olares Headscale registration path
Read-only SSH access to the Olares host if access recovery succeeds
constrained_outputs: A dated handoff recording whether the private-mesh access path was restored or remained blocked
If access is restored, a read-only host runtime inventory for Docker, K3s or Helm, installed apps, ports, volumes, networks, and private-lane timers
A statement of whether the runtime-inventory portion of Packet 001 is now satisfied within this packet
A statement of whether VS Code Remote-SSH is newly viable or remains blocked
No installs, no promotions, no ingress changes, no auth changes, no migration, no AI-toolchain rollout, no hosting changes
validation_steps: Use the local TermiPass named-pipe API to recover LarePass with ControlURL=https://headscale.jlswen2121.olares.com and WantRunning=true.
Confirm pending node-key registration in the Olares Headscale pod for user default if recovery requires it.
Validate BackendState Running and workstation mesh IP allocation in the 100.64.* range.
Validate non-interactive SSH to olares@100.64.0.1 succeeds.
On success, immediately capture read-only host runtime inventory for Docker, K3s or Helm, installed apps, ports, volumes, networks, and private-lane timers.
If access recovery fails, record the exact blocker and stop without widening scope.
Do not install or modify services, do not change ingress or auth posture, and do not alter public-host SSH trust while executing this packet.
sql_permission_note: This packet authorizes workstation-side access recovery and read-only host inventory only. It does not authorize database writes, runtime mutation, new Olares installs, AI-toolchain scaffolding, ingress changes, or hosting changes.
closure_note: Executed on 2026-05-03 and closed blocked. The TermiPass named-pipe local API was reachable, but local status remained `BackendState: NeedsLogin` with zero node key, no auth URL, no mesh IP, and no usable `100.64.*` route even after bounded preference, start, and login-interactive recovery attempts. Because private-mesh access was not restored, Headscale registration was not possible from this path, host runtime was not directly inspected, and the runtime-inventory portion of Packet 001 remains unsatisfied. The result did not materially change the live Olares boundary.
next_action: Do not run a Claude Code runtime-reconciliation pass from this packet result. Keep the current Step 3 decision surface in force unless a later bounded packet produces materially new access or host-runtime evidence.
